item.title: What Was Clavicular?
item.description: The internet’s most famous looks-maxxer is far more pernicious than he may seem.
item.dc:creator: Will Gottsegen

item.title: E-Verify Is No Silver Bullet
item.description: Focusing enforcement on employers might be the easiest choice in immigration policy—just as soon as you make all of the hard ones.
item.dc:creator: Marc Novicoff

item.title: Protecting a Hero Too Long
item.description: The pressure to shield men who hurt women
item.dc:creator: Jenisha Watts

item.title: Meet the New ICE
item.dc:creator: Hanna Rosin

item.title: OpenAI Is Doing Everything … Poorly
item.description: The company’s sudden decision to pull the plug on Sora is a sign of deeper trouble.
item.dc:creator: Lila Shroff

item.title: RFK Jr. Is Losing His Grip on the CDC
item.description: The Trump administration seems to be putting MAHA on notice.
item.dc:creator: Tom Bartlett

item.title: Trump Is Asking to Be Bailed Out Again
item.description: The president’s eagerness to act keeps getting him into difficult spots—which he then demands that legislators and the public help him escape.
item.dc:creator: David A. Graham

item.title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: A Little Dickens
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for some extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: When Claude Met Claude
item.description: Why is Anthropic sponsoring an exhibition about Monet?
item.dc:creator: Matteo Wong

item.title: A Legal Decision That Could Change Social Media
item.description: Jurors found Meta and Google liable for building apps that inflicted mental-health problems on a teenager, and similar lawsuits are on the horizon.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlyn Tiffany

item.title: The War With Iran Is Exposing Big Problems for the Military
item.description: What we have learned about the strengths and weaknesses of the American way of war
item.dc:creator: Eliot A. Cohen

item.title: Scenes From the Canadian Arctic
item.description: Carlos Osorio, a photojournalist with Reuters, recently traveled to Canada’s northern reaches to document military exercises, daily life, robotic testing, wildlife, and more.
item.dc:creator: Alan Taylor

item.title: The Far-Right Algorithm: Anti-Churchill, Anti-West
item.description: The historian Andrew Roberts on why many right-wing podcasters now believe that the wrong side won the Second World War, and the rise of algorithmically driven pseudo-historians. Plus: Trump is looking for an off-ramp from his war in Iran, and Gore Vidal’s novelBurr.
item.dc:creator: David Frum

item.title: The U.S. and Iran Are Fighting a Massively Asymmetrical War
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz presents a classic war theater for an insurgency to bog down superior forces.
item.dc:creator: Nancy A. Youssef

item.title: Trump Owes Mueller
item.description: The president may resent the former special counsel, but he is also indebted to him.
item.dc:creator: David Frum

item.title: How AI Is Creeping IntoThe New York Times
item.description: Artificial intelligence seems to be turning up, undisclosed, in the opinion pages of major news publications.
item.dc:creator: Vauhini Vara

item.title: Twenty Seconds of ‘Task Saturation’ at LaGuardia
item.description: Having two controllers on a midnight shift might be standard procedure, but they can still be overwhelmed.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Mondor

item.title: How to Guess If Your Job Will Exist in Five Years
item.description: Ask yourself: Are you coal, or are you a horse?
item.dc:creator: Annie Lowrey

item.title: The Deep Risk That Republican Hawks Overlooked
item.description: If the Iran war goes badly, the isolationist, anti-Israel wing of the party is likely to steer the GOP’s future.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Chait

item.title: Where Are All the Campus Protests?
item.description: Two years ago, students occupied buildings and colonized the quad. Now the same places are strangely silent.
item.dc:creator: Rose Horowitch
